issue: Unbounded memory growth in per-email rate-limit dict. Buckets are only pruned per-key on access; orphaned email keys persist forever.
Same unbounded-growth pattern. Entries only TTL-expire on lookup. Need periodic sweep or LRU bound.
Status code 'nan' is overloaded: returned both when vol is NaN and when sharpe is NaN. Split into 'nan_vol' / 'nan_sharpe' so the data_quality channel can distinguish them.
Records the attempt before verification runs. Failed verifications still consume budget. Acceptable from abuse-prevention angle; docstring should document this.
In the outer except, _fail() runs an UPDATE that may itself raise. If Supabase is down, the original exception is masked by the Supabase error and the analytics row is stuck in 'computing'.
update_payload includes data_quality column added in migration 20260516121000. If deploy lands code before migration, UPDATE fails with column-not-found. Document deploy ordering in migration header.
